Office of the Law Revision Counsel
Payment to widows and heirs of deceased Members of Congress
For a payment to the widow, widower, or heirs of Members who have died, $174,000 for each such Member....

For salaries and expenses of the House of Representatives, $1,516,673,000, of which $890,000 shall be available for official mail costs, $25,000,000 shall be available for franked mail costs of the House, and $100,000 shall be available for the House Page Program.
For the Office of the Inspector General, $5,000,000.
For expenses of the Joint Economic Committee, $4,203,000.
For expenses of the Joint Committee on Taxation, $12,804,000.

For medical supplies, equipment, and expenses of the emergency rooms, and for the Attending Physician and his assistants, including: -1 an allowance of $2,000 per month to the Attending Physician; -2 an allowance of $1,000 per month to one senior assistant; -3 an allowance of $725 per month each to three assistants; -4 an allowance of $580 per month each to two assistants; and -5 an allowance of $400 per month to one assistant, $4,496,000.

For salaries of employees of the Capitol Police, including overtime, hazardous duty differential, and Government contributions for health, retirement, social security, and other applicable employee benefits, $515,000,000, to be disbursed by the Chief of the Capitol Police or his designee.

For necessary expenses of the Capitol Police, including motor vehicles, communications and other equipment, security equipment and installation thereof, uniforms, weapons, supplies, materials, training, medical services, forensic services, stenographic services, personal and professional services, the employee assistance program, the awards program, postage, communication services, travel advances, relocation of instructor and liaison personnel for the Federal Law Enforcement Training Center, and not more than $5,000 for public relations and advertising, $131,000,000, to be disbursed by the Chief of the Capitol Police or his designee: Provided, That, notwithstanding any other provision of law, the cost of basic training for the Capitol Police at the Federal Law Enforcement Training Center for fiscal year 2025 shall be paid by the Secretary of Homeland Security from funds available to the Department of Homeland Security.

For salaries and expenses of the Office of Compliance, as authorized by section 305 of the Congressional Accountability Act of 1995 (2 U.S.C. 1385), $7,556,000: Provided, That not more than $1,000 may be expended on the certification of the Executive Director of the Office of Compliance in connection with official representation and reception expenses.
For salaries and expenses necessary for operation of the Congressional Budget Office, including not more than $6,000 for official reception and representation expenses, $57,232,000.

For salaries for the Architect of the Capitol, and other personal services, at rates of pay provided by law; for surveys and studies in connection with activities under the care of the Architect of the Capitol; for all necessary expenses for the general and administrative support of the activities under the care of the Architect of the Capitol including the Botanic Garden; electrical and mechanical care and maintenance of the Capitol, the congressional office buildings, the Library of Congress buildings, the Supreme Court building, the Thurgood Marshall Federal Judiciary Building, the Capitol Power Plant, the Capitol Police headquarters, and the Capitol grounds; care and maintenance of the grounds of the Library of Congress, the Supreme Court building, the Thurgood Marshall Federal Judiciary Building, and the United States Botanic Garden; expenses of the Department of Arborist, and expenses of the House Office Building Commission; for purchase or exchange, maintenance, and operation of a passenger motor vehicle; and not more than $5,000 for official reception and representation expenses, $161,716,000, of which $19,714,000 shall remain available until September 30, 2027.
For all necessary expenses for the maintenance, care and operation of the Capitol, $36,136,000, of which $12,136,000 shall remain available until September 30, 2027.

For all necessary expenses for care and improvement of grounds surrounding the Capitol, the Senate and House office buildings, and the Capitol Power Plant, $16,691,000, of which $4,691,000 shall remain available until September 30, 2027.

For all necessary expenses for the maintenance, care and operation of Senate office buildings; and furniture and furnishings to be expended under the control and supervision of the Architect of the Capitol, $96,378,000, of which $21,378,000 shall remain available until September 30, 2027.
For all necessary expenses for the maintenance, care and operation of the House office buildings, $199,263,000, of which $71,263,000 shall remain available until September 30, 2027.

For all necessary expenses for the maintenance, care and operation of the Capitol Power Plant; lighting, heating, power (including the purchase of electrical energy) and water and sewer services for the Capitol, Senate and House office buildings, Library of Congress buildings, Supreme Court building, Thurgood Marshall Federal Judiciary Building, Capitol Police headquarters, the Capitol, Capitol grounds, and the Botanic Garden; heating the Government Publishing Office and Washington City Post Office, and heating and chilled water for air conditioning for the Union Station complex; delivery of steam and chilled water to the Folger Shakespeare Library; and for stands and other facilities on or near the Capitol grounds as may be requisite for the Congress in connection with the inauguration of the President-elect; as authorized by the Act of March 3, 1921, $137,800,000, of which $32,800,000 shall remain available until September 30, 2027.

For all necessary expenses for the mechanical and structural maintenance, care and operation of the Library of Congress buildings and grounds, $67,913,000, of which $17,913,000 shall remain available until September 30, 2027.
Capitol Police buildings, grounds and security

For all necessary expenses for the maintenance, care and operation of Capitol Police buildings, grounds and security enhancements, $62,656,000, of which $32,656,000 shall remain available until September 30, 2027: Provided, That none of the funds made available under this heading shall be used for the design, construction, operation, or maintenance of a United States Capitol Police Training Facility: Provided further, That the Architect of the Capitol shall transfer to the Chief of the Capitol Police funds made available under this heading for expenses incurred in connection with training Capitol Police personnel.

For all necessary expenses for the maintenance, care and operation of the Botanic Garden and the nurseries, buildings, grounds, and collections; and purchase and exchange, maintenance, repair, and operation of a passenger motor vehicle; all under the direction of the Joint Committee on the Library, $21,200,000, of which $2,200,000 shall remain available until September 30, 2027: Provided, That, of the amount made available under this heading, the Architect may obligate and expend such sums as may be necessary for the maintenance, care and operation of the National Garden established under section 307E of the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 1989 (2 U.S.C. 2146), 22 upon vouchers approved by the Architect or a duly authorized designee.
For all necessary expenses for the operation of the Capitol Visitor Center, $27,103,000.

For necessary expenses of the Library of Congress not otherwise provided for, including development and maintenance of the Library's catalogs; custody and custodial care of the Library buildings; special clothing; cleaning, laundering and repair of uniforms; preservation of motion pictures in the custody of the Library; operation and maintenance of the American Folklife Center in the Library; preparation and distribution of catalog records and other publications of the Library; hire or purchase of one passenger motor vehicle; and expenses of the Library of Congress Trust Fund Board not properly chargeable to the income of any trust fund held by the Board, $569,956,000, of which not more than $6,000,000 shall be derived from collections credited to this appropriation during fiscal year 2025, and shall remain available until expended, under the Act of June 28, 1902 (chapter 1301; 32 Stat. 480; 2 U.S.C. 150) and not more than $350,000 shall be derived from fees and other collections: Provided, That the Library of Congress may not obligate or expend any funds derived from collections under the Act of June 28, 1902, in excess of the amount authorized for obligation or expenditure in appropriations Acts: Provided further, That the total amount available for obligation shall be reduced by the amount by which collections are less than $6,350,000: Provided further, That of the total amount appropriated, not more than $12,000 may be expended, on the certification of the Librarian of Congress, in connection with official representation and reception expenses: Provided further, That of the total amount appropriated, $8,718,000 shall be transferred to the Abraham Lincoln Bicentennial Commission for expenses of the Commission under Public Law 106-173, of which $2,000,000 shall be available for design and construction of exhibits.
